#threatreport #LowCompleteness Active Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Environment Management | 12-06-2026 Source: kudelskisecurity.com/researc… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Shinyhunters (🧠motivation: information_theft)  💀Threats: Meshcentral_tool, Meshagent_tool,  🎯Victims: Oracle peoplesoft deployments, Organizations with internet accessible peoplesoft administrative interfaces  🔓CVEs: CVE-2026-35273  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2026-352…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: True     Soft:  - oracle peoplesoft_enterprise_peopletools (8.61, 8.62)  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1016, T1018, T1021.004, T1036.005, T1059.004, T1082, T1083, T1110.003, T1190, T1219, ...  🧨IOCs:  - File: 7  - IP: 5  💽Software: WebLogic  #threatreport: Threat actor UNC6240, known as ShinyHunters, is currently conducting an active exploitation campaign targeting Oracle PeopleSoft systems, specifically the Environment Management component (EMHub/PSEMHUB). This campaign has been linked to the exploitation of a critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability, identified as CVE-2026-35273, which has a CVSS score of 9.8. The exploitation activity was observed between May 27 and June 9, 2026, prior to an official advisory from Oracle, indicating that it was a zero-day vulnerability.  Systems most affected include Oracle PeopleSoft deployments with EMHub enabled, particularly those with exposed endpoints like /PSEMHUB/hub and /PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector. Compromised systems include installations running PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools versions 8.61 and 8.62 that have not been patched against this vulnerability. Upon successful exploitation, attackers gain unauthorized access, enabling them to execute code remotely on vulnerable servers, which allows further intrusion into the targeted environments.  Post-compromise, the attackers establish persistence through the deployment of customized MeshCentral agents hidden as legitimate Microsoft Azure services. These agents, utilizing innocuous filenames, connect to attacker-controlled command-and-control infrastructure, facilitating continued access and control over the compromised systems. The attackers then engage in extensive reconnaissance, examining configuration files and network details to create a comprehensive view of the victim's infrastructure and to identify additional targets for lateral movement.  The campaign showcases advanced lateral movement techniques, particularly through the use of a custom propagation script, victim_abbreviation_fanout.sh. This script automates credential spraying using SSH against discovered internal hosts with predefined administrative credentials. Successful authentications execute a defacement and extortion marker that signals the compromise and enables further propagation of the attacker’s influence across the network. Additionally, the attackers prepare for data exfiltration, compressing stolen files before transferring them to an external site linked with the ShinyHunters Data Leak Site, reinforcing their extortion tactics.  The infrastructure used by the attackers indicates an organized approach to the command-and-control operations, with evidence of MeshCentral installations, automated TLS certificate provisioning, and a suite of command-line utilities to carry out various administrative tasks across the compromised landscape. Furthermore, attacker command histories reveal systematic reconnaissance and data exfiltration methodologies, emphasizing the structured nature of this intrusion campaign.  Organizations utilizing Oracle PeopleSoft are strongly advised to promptly implement updates addressing CVE-2026-35273 and carefully monitor their logs for suspicious activities, particularly in relation to unauthorized HTTP requests and potential server-side request forgery.

Sources for the above: BleepingComputer's Nissan disclosure (June 29, 2026) — bleepingcomputer.com/news/se… — is the primary incident report. Oracle's CVE-2026-35273 advisory with emergency mitigation guidance lives at oracle.com/security-alerts/a…. ShinyHunters attribution and the education-sector victim analysis are confirmed by Mandiant via Google Cloud Threat Intelligence at cloud.google.com/blog/topics…. CVE-2026-35273 is not yet indexed in CISA KEV as of 22:05 UTC June 29.

Nissan Americas disclosed a breach today that isn't really about Nissan. It's about Oracle PeopleSoft, a thirteen-day zero-day window, and at least 100 organizations that had no patch to apply and no advisory to action while ShinyHunters worked methodically through every internet-facing PeopleSoft instance they could find.  CVE-2026-35273 is an unauthenticated remote exploitation vector in Oracle PeopleSoft PeopleTools. ShinyHunters knew about it — and were running it — from May 27 through June 9. Oracle disclosed it after that window closed. That's the timeline that matters. The blast radius reflects it: 300+ breached instances claimed, 100+ organizations already notified by Mandiant, victims confirmed across education, financial services, and automotive. Nissan's Americas footprint covers current and former employees in the US, Canada, Mexico, and Brazil. SSNs, SINs, national IDs, banking information, payroll and tax records, dependent data. The complete employee PII dossier. Thirteen days. No patch available.  Mandiant confirmed the attribution. Nissan was specifically targeted, not swept up incidentally — Oracle's own breach notification to the California AG states this. The reason isn't hard to reconstruct: ShinyHunters has a pattern of identifying high-value targets within a broader campaign when HR and payroll data is particularly monetizable. Four countries of Nissan Americas HR records fit that profile exactly.  The monetization phase is already running. Data from Nottingham University and NAIC has leaked on the ShinyHunters site. Instructure, which runs Canvas, paid a ransom to stop their leak. Nissan hasn't disclosed whether they received an extortion demand. The pattern implies one has been issued or is coming.  This is the third major SaaS HR campaign in ShinyHunters' recent operational history — after Snowflake in 2024 and the Salesforce/Drift integration work before that. The playbook is coherent and it's maturing: find a widely-deployed enterprise SaaS platform, acquire or exploit a high-value vulnerability, breach at volume, selectively extort targets with the most monetizable data, leak others to establish credibility. Industrialized data theft, not opportunistic. The infosec drinking game continues — enterprise HR platforms are the crown jewel, and we keep leaving them in the lobby.  On the MITRE side, this maps cleanly: T1190 for the PeopleSoft zero-day initial access, T1078 for post-exploitation lateral movement inside the application, T1213 for HR and payroll record exfiltration, T1567 for staging and exfil, T1657 for the extortion layer. Notably, T1486 — data encryption for impact — is not observed. This is pure data theft and extortion. No ransomware component.  CVE-2026-35273 is not yet in CISA KEV as of this writing. Given confirmed in-wild exploitation at scale, active extortion campaigns across multiple named sectors, and Mandiant's public confirmation, that listing is hours to days away. If you're running Oracle PeopleSoft and you were internet-facing during that May 27–June 9 window, the patch window for zero-day exposure is closed. Oracle has released emergency mitigations. Apply them now. Don't wait for the KEV entry to make it official — the window has been open, and it has names attached to it.

CVE-2021-31956 is a Windows kernel privilege escalation vulnerability in NTFS ("ntfs.sys") caused by an integer underflow while processing Extended Attributes (EA). The resulting paged pool corruption can be turned into arbitrary kernel read/write primitives.  What makes NCC Group's research particularly interesting is that it demonstrates exploitation without relying on the CVE-2021-31955 kernel address leak. Instead, it leverages the Windows Notification Framework (WNF) to build reliable exploitation primitives, making it an excellent study of NTFS internals, WNF, kernel pool corruption, and modern Windows exploit development.

sample_tweets.text: Three Breaches. One Pattern. Delayed Detection.  Three significant breach disclosures and each revealing the same underlying gap: detection and notification came far too late.  - Nissan North America discovered a breach of its Oracle PeopleSoft HR systems. Social Security numbers, tax data, and financial information were exposed for employees across the U.S., Canada, Mexico, and Brazil. Nissan was one of "hundreds" of Oracle customers affected.  - AssuranceAmerica is notifying more than 506,000 customers of a March cyberattack that exposed personal and insurance claims data months after the incident occurred.  - Yellow Trucking Company disclosed a March 2025 breach of former employee personal, financial, and medical data 15 months after it happened.  Three industries. Three different attack vectors. One common thread: orgs discovering breaches after a long time.  Third-party HR and ERP systems are high-value targets precisely because they aggregate sensitive data across large populations. Without continuous monitoring of identity and data plane activity, these breaches go undetected for months. The floor just shifted under digital surveillance law. The Supreme Court held 6-3 today in Chatrie v. United States (No. 25-112) that geofence warrants — government demands for location data tied to a place, not a named suspect — are Fourth Amendment searches. Justice Kagan wrote the majority. She wrote it broad on purpose.  This is the most consequential Fourth Amendment ruling since Carpenter (2018), and the coalition tells you it has legs: Roberts and Kavanaugh joined the three liberal justices. Gorsuch concurred separately on a property theory — location data as "papers and effects" — which hands privacy advocates a second legal theory to argue from. That 6-3 alignment, crossing the Court's usual partisan geometry, is a signal this isn't a one-term accident.  The case originated with a 2019 bank robbery in Virginia. Police pulled a geofence warrant from Google's Sensorvault database and identified Okello Chatrie without ever naming him in the initial request. The Court declined to rule on whether that specific warrant was valid. What it ruled on — unambiguously — is the constitutional question: this is a search, and it requires warrant-level justification.  Kagan didn't write a geofence-only opinion. She wrote a technology-agnostic framework. The key line from the majority: "A new technology should not transform what individuals had reasonably thought they could withhold from the Government." That framing is not limited to Sensorvault. It reaches browser-history requests, AI inference logs, fitness app data, smart speaker transcripts, and any reverse-attribute warrant — show me who was in this dataset matching these characteristics. The government cannot exploit the architecture of a new technology to reach data people reasonably expected to keep private. The scope of that sentence is the whole ballgame.  The immediate irony: Google already moved. The company restructured its location data architecture before the ruling came down, which means the largest original target is now effectively moot — Sensorvault as a government tool is offline. The ruling now lands on every other company that hasn't done that cleanup. Meta's precise location layer. Apple's location APIs. Tile, Life360, carrier location records sold to aggregators, data brokers. And the emerging class of AI systems that build location-correlated behavioral profiles. The ACLU's counsel noted law enforcement and courts are on notice. Google gets the clarity dividend without the compliance hangover.  Alito's dissent is the tell on where the fight goes next. He called the majority's reach "seismic" and warned of "debris" to clean up for the foreseeable future. That language is a roadmap — expect DOJ to push warrant-specific carveouts in the lower courts over the next 18 months. The ruling is settled; the edges aren't.  On markets, the read is more interesting than the headline numbers suggest. GOOGL is up +4.61% today ($352.93). META +2.83% ($565.85). The ruling creates compliance costs, not existential liability, for platforms with scale legal teams — and regulatory certainty is preferable to open-ended exposure. Google already made the architectural call; it's now shielded. Meta faces harder work; its location layer hasn't been restructured in the same way. AMZN is up +3.27% ($240.31) but Alexa location data and AWS inference APIs now face new scrutiny. AAPL is down -0.59% ($282.11) — privacy-first architecture is already aligned, but compliance overhead is real. MSFT is off -1.30% ($368.11); Azure and Bing location inference pipelines need review. The broader public tech rally (QQQ +2.4%, SPY +1.6%) is macro-driven. Chatrie is a tailwind for regulatory clarity, not the driver.  The real risk concentrates where it usually does — smaller data brokers, location aggregators, and AI companies that haven't done the Sensorvault-equivalent cleanup. They're mostly private. Nobody's pricing that today.  Three direct exposure vectors for any company operating location-aware AI pipelines: First, agentic inference systems that ingest, retain, or infer location-correlated user data — even indirectly through behavioral modeling — now sit atop data that carries Fourth Amendment protection. Chatrie doesn't just protect the user; it constrains government access at the platform layer. Second, third-party data vendor review is no longer optional. The third-party doctrine erosion that began with Carpenter accelerates here; your enrichment vendor's compliance posture is now your liability. Third, any government request for location-correlated user data going forward requires a warrant. Legal response protocols need to be updated explicitly. That last one is where the analyst stops and the lawyers start.  The MITRE ATT&CK mapping is worth noting for the agentic-AI layer: T1430 (Location Tracking) — government requests for AI-system location inferences now require warrants. T1119 (Automated Collection) — reverse-attribute queries are Fourth Amendment searches. T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) — AI-generated behavioral profiles tied to location face new warrant requirements. T1056.003 (Web Portal Capture) — platform-side location data retained for AI training is now a surveillance target requiring legal process.  Carpenter cracked the third-party doctrine. Chatrie widens that crack across the entire technology stack. The companies that read the room early, restructured their data architecture, and treated user location data as a liability rather than an asset just got validated by the highest court in the country. The ones that didn't are running behind schedule. It always does work out this way, eventually.

做 AI 想搞钱、又不想从零造概念的看这条:今天 GitHub 涨得最猛的一批,几乎全是"给 AI 配料"的——记忆、品味、技能包、官方插件。模型谁都追不动,但给模型铺路的活,一个人就能接。我挑了 7 个正在涨的,每个说清是什么、强在哪、你能顺着它做个什么。  1/ Understand-Anything(Egonex-AI/Understand-Anything)⭐6.9万 今日+4,697。这条赢在"势头"——今天涨得最猛的就是它。它把任何一坨代码变成一张能探索、能搜索、能直接提问的交互式知识图谱,Claude Code、Cursor、Copilot、Gemini CLI 都接得上。所有人都在用 AI 写代码,但一进大项目就抓瞎:AI 读得了单文件,讲不清整个仓库怎么咬合。它把这道坎做成了能看的图。顺着它做个什么:做一个托管版,挂到团队的代码库上、每次 PR 自动把图谱刷新,卖"新人一天上手老项目";或者针对某个流行框架/某个行业的祖传系统做专版,把"读懂这套代码"打包成订阅。github.com/Egonex-AI/Underst…  2/ taste-skill(Leonxlnx/taste-skill)⭐5.3万 今日+1,430。这条胜在"传播力"——它戳的是所有人都受够了的那件事:AI 写出来的东西又平又套。它是一个给 AI"装品味"的 skill,专治那种一眼假的通用废话。skill 这种形态本身就传得快、还好改。顺着它做个什么:做"品牌嗓音"包——把某个品牌、某个行业的腔调(法律但有人味、奢侈品、开发者那种冷幽默)调成一个专属 taste skill 卖;或者干脆做一个品味档案的市场,谁都能挑一套自己想要的调性装上。github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-sk…  3/ claude-mem(thedotmack/claude-mem)⭐8.5万 今日+352。这条的看点是"护城河深"。它给 agent 装了跨会话的记忆:把这次干了什么自动抓下来、用 AI 压缩、下次再把相关的上下文喂回去,Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Copilot 通吃。agent 最大的尴尬就是"每次都失忆",它把这事补上了。顺着它做个什么:做记忆即服务——托管一份、团队共享的记忆、一家公司的知识能跨人跨项目地攒下来。真正的护城河不是代码,是越用越厚的那份记忆数据,别人抄得走工具抄不走积累。github.com/thedotmack/claude…  4/ knowledge-work-plugins(anthropics/knowledge-work-plugins)⭐2.2万 今日+1,718。这条最值钱的是"卡位"——这是 Anthropic 官方刚开的、给知识工作者在 Claude Cowork 里用的插件仓库。官方亲自下场、地基刚浇,但只铺了基础几样,财务结账、法务收件、招聘初筛这些细分场景几乎是空地。顺着它做个什么:照着官方格式做一个"把某类知识工作做顺"的插件去卖——官方分发的口子刚开,谁先把一个垂直活做扎实,谁就吃第一波生态红利。这是今天这批里,给"想现在就搞钱"的人最实在的一个位子。github.com/anthropics/knowle…  5/ Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills(mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills)⭐2.3万 今日+880。这条赢在"可建、还能照搬"。它是 754 个结构化的网络安全 skill,对齐了 MITRE ATT&CK、NIST 这些框架,走 agentskills.io 标准,20 多个平台通用。它证明了一件事:把一个受监管领域的专业知识,按一个公认标准做成 skill 包,是立得住的形态。顺着它做个什么:把同一套打法搬到另一个合规重的垂直——医疗 HIPAA、金融 SOC2、隐私 GDPR——做一套对齐标准的 skill 包,再配一个托管的自动审计,卖给那个圈子里没人手做合规的小团队。github.com/mukul975/Anthropi…  6/ twenty(twentyhq/twenty)⭐5.2万 今日+216。换个口味,这条不在 skill 圈:它是开源版的 Salesforce,而且是冲着 AI 重做的。顺着它做个什么——别去重造一个,坐在它上面做生意:给某个垂直(房产中介、代运营机构、诊所)配好一套开箱即用的 CRM 模板,或者做一个托管版,带"从 Salesforce 搬家"+ 上手陪跑。跟当年靠托管 WordPress、托管开源数据库赚钱是同一个套路:底层有人替你养,你卖上面那层"省心"。github.com/twentyhq/twenty  7/ OpenStock(Open-Dev-Society/OpenStock)⭐1.3万 今日+156。这条胜在"发散"——它是贵价行情平台的开源平替:实时价格、个性化提醒、公司洞察,永久免费。免费开源本身不赚钱,但它给你省掉了从零搭的成本。顺着它做个什么:托管它,然后把"便利"卖成订阅——更快的数据源、更聪明的提醒规则、组合分析;或者只服务一个细分(某类资产、某个市场)做深。开源做内核、收钱靠托管和省心,经典开放内核打法。github.com/Open-Dev-Society/…  只挑一个上手,我推 knowledge-work-plugins。理由是它踩在一个"刚开、又一定会变大"的位子上:官方亲自下场把知识工作的插件生态铺开了,需求正在涨(用 Cowork 的人都在等好插件),但细分场景几乎是空地。你不用造新概念、不用养模型,顺着官方的格式和分发,把某一个知识工作的活(对账、合同初审、招聘筛人随便一个)做扎实,就能接住第一波生态红利。今天这批里,它是离"现在就能收钱"最近的那个。

Our greatest threat is failed education.  It no longer teaches young Americans how to think—how systems work, why dispersed power matters, the importance of merit, or the civic discipline of “Trust but Verify.”  Instead, it teaches grievance, identity-based outcomes, distrust of merit, and blind trust in authority. This destroys verification capacity exactly when we need it most. If we fail to fix education, everything else we do will have a short shelf life.  The Predatory Threats Two ideologies exploit this gap because they cannot coexist with pluralistic dispersed power. They use our weakened institutions as bridges, then eliminate them.  Communism (in its modern progressive and socialist forms) uses grievance politics, equity mandates, and regulatory expansion as bridges toward centralized control.  Political Islam is the clearer doctrinal threat. Political Islam’s foundational doctrine demands total religious law and permits concealment and strategic patience—producing an asymmetric verification gap that pluralism is structurally unequipped to handle. Bridge ideologies serve its expansion until critical mass is reached and the bridges are burned.  If we don’t fix our broken education system, all other reforms will be short lived.

You don’t amass millions by selling cheap words and empty promises—that’s not a ministry, it’s a predatory scam. This is textbook cultishness, and cults are never democratic. Freedom of speech there is entirely one-way: you obey, or you are silenced. All these self-proclaimed prophets are illegitimate fraudsters and criminals. In saner countries, these syndicates are banned. When even educated people like youfall prey, it proves just how dangerously powerful these cult leaders are. No one is safe. The ultimate tragedy of a cult is that the brainwashed victims will vigorously defend their own oppressors and cruelly recruit others, exactly as you are doing. Stop trying to drag independent minds into your psychological mess. How Modern Prophetic Cults Operate To understand why even highly educated people get trapped, it helps to look at the specific machinery modern "prophetic" ministries use. They don't rely on simple faith; they use sophisticated psychological manipulation. 1. The Manufactured Crisis and the "Paid" Cure Cult leaders create an environment where every life problem—financial hardship, illness, or relationship trouble—is framed as a spiritual attack or a generational curse. The only way to break it? "Seeding" money. They commodify God, convincing vulnerable people that the size of their financial sacrifice dictates the size of their breakthrough. 2. The Illusion of Supernatural Access Prophets build absolute authority by performing carefully staged, "prophetic" revelations (often using basic social media scraping, cold reading, or information fed by minders before the service). By revealing intimate details about a follower's life, they establish a terrifying illusion of omniscience. Once a follower believes the prophet speaks directly for God, the prophet's words become immune to criticism. 3. Exploiting Intellectual Vulnerability There is a common myth that only the uneducated join cults. In reality, highly educated people are frequently targeted because they are often under high stress, carry immense professional pressure, or are seeking absolute answers in an uncertain world. Cult leaders exploit this by offering absolute certainty. 4. Weaponized Isolation and Shared Trauma Modern prophetic churches create an "us vs. them" mentality. Anyone who questions the prophet—even close family—is labeled "demonic," a "hater," or an "enemy of progress." Followers are systematically isolated from external support networks until the church becomes their entire social, financial, and emotional reality. 5. Turning Victims into Recruiters Once a follower has invested significant time and money, ego-defense mechanisms kick in. Admitting they were scammed is too painful to bear. To justify their own exploitation, they aggressively defend the leader and recruit others—seeking external validation to soothe their own internal, subconscious doubts. @bla_bidza  Actually you are very contradicting because politicians are better than cults

Your Balance Updated. The Blockchain Never Did.  On AscendEX, That Gap Is Where a Lot of People's Money Currently Lives.  For weeks, AscendEX users have described the same sequence: They request a withdrawal, the balance leaves their account, and then nothing happens. No transaction hash, no on chain movement, no coins arriving.   The exchange's own help center promises a TXID within two hours. Some users say they have waited days, a few say more than a month, with funds frozen in an "initiating" state that never resolves.  On June 26, on chain investigator @zachxbt put a name to the unease.  He reviewed AscendEX's known hot wallets on Arkham and TRM and found them thin on the assets people actually deposit, ETH, USDT, USDC, SOL, and weighted instead toward illiquid small caps.  His read was measured and pointed at once, the exchange is likely facing liquidity issues, and no one should be depositing to it.  🔹What the Missing Hash Actually Means🔹  A withdrawal with no TXID is the tell.  Your balance is a number in the exchange's private database, the coins are a separate pool the exchange custodies.  When the two diverge, you are not waiting on the blockchain, you are waiting on the exchange's permission to move its own reserves.   ZachXBT was careful here, and so everyone should be.  Thin hot wallets are operational liquidity, not the whole balance sheet, and most of a healthy exchange sits in cold storage that is not publicly tagged.  👉 This is not proof of insolvency, it is proof of strain and of opacity.  The trouble is you cannot tell those apart from the outside, and no proof of reserves has been published since mid 2025.  🔹The Permission Layer, in Its Purest Form🔹  I keep returning to the control layer welded into modern infrastructure. Circle can freeze a stablecoin, a staking wrapper can gate your yield, a prediction market can resolve against you.  A centralized exchange is the original version of that idea, and the cleanest (or dirtiest?).  ➡️ You deposited a permissionless asset and received an IOU and a dashboard.  The withdrawal button is the off switch, and it was never yours.  The most decentralized money ever built, parked behind the most centralized chokepoint in the stack. Meanwhile the official account keeps posting fresh token listings. Business as usual on the timeline, initiating on the withdrawals.  🔹The Script the Industry Knows by Heart🔹  We have watched this sequence before.  Delays, generic support replies, users muted in the group, reserves questioned, then a clearer picture that is sometimes benign and sometimes a crater.  Mt Gox, FTX, and a row of mid tier names taught one lesson, and it was always about custody rather than the coin.  This may resolve cleanly. AscendEX survived a 78 million dollar Lazarus hack in 2021 and compensated users. It may also not.  The point is that you, the depositor, have no way to know, and that not knowing is the product you accepted the moment you handed over the keys.  🔹Actionable NFA for Wise Investors🔹  1️⃣ If your funds are on AscendEX, stop depositing, try small test withdrawals on the most liquid networks, document every ticket, and watch the tagged wallets on Arkham yourself.  Cancelling a stuck request has returned funds to spot balance for some.  2️⃣ Treat any "recovery service" that messages you as a second scam stacked on the first.  They always swarm these moments, and no honest party will DM you to retrieve your money.  3️⃣ The structural lesson outlives one exchange. Counterparty risk is not hedged by a bigger logo, it is hedged by self custody and by venues that publish live, verifiable reserves.   The assets you own on your wallet - with no withdrawal queue and no support ticket is the entire reason this technology exists.  A blockchain exists so a withdrawal needs no one's permission.  The moment it needs permission, you were never really on the blockchain... bro.

As someone actually working in security analysis, my take is completely different you missed the real  point. yes, AI models are getting better at finding vulnerabilities in code. yes, they're making app security teams more efficient. that part is real.  BUT here's what everyone's ignoring: the same AI tools attackers are using are getting just as capable. and unlike defenders who follow rules, attackers don't have guardrails. ransomware hit record highs in 2024 with 44% of all breaches involving ransomware and 75% of system-intrusion breaches. breach frequency isn't going down. it's accelerating.  state-sponsored attackers (China, Russia, North Korea), organized ransomware groups, and well-funded threat actors all have access to the same AI tools now. they're using them for reconnaissance, exploit generation, credential theft, and lateral movement. AI-driven attacks are growing faster than defenses can scale.  so here's the reality: app security getting more efficient? cool. but that's ONE part of the job. the real bottleneck isn't finding bugs in code. it's defending the entire attack surface. and that's exploding.  we need people in: • SOC teams (monitoring millions of alerts) • Identity and access management (blocking lateral movement) • Supply chain security (vendors are the weakest link) • Incident response (forensics, recovery, investigation) • Cloud security (misconfigurations are everywhere) • Threat intelligence (understanding what's coming) • Phishing and social engineering defense (humans are still the entry point) • DevSecOps (shifting security left in development)  DevSecOps market alone is projected to grow from $3.73B in 2024 to $41.66B by 2030   companies are most vulnerable because they're trying to patch everything at once. people are most vulnerable because attackers are using AI-generated phishing at scale. phishing emails generated by AI are indistinguishable from real ones, and 40% of breaches start with email . the shortage isn't in app developers and bug hunters and in red teaming. it's on the DEFENSE side.  we have way more people learning to code and build products than learning to defend them. so AI didn't kill cybersecurity jobs. it changed the game. offense got a turbo boost. defense is still manually triaging alerts. the real demand is for defenders, and we don't have nearly enough. SOC analysts, incident responders, threat hunters, security architects - these roles are wide open and understaffed. if you're learning cybersecurity in 2026, don't worry about AI replacing you with this cheap models. worry about choosing the right side of the equation.

sample_tweets.text: "홍명보 감독의 사례에서 봤듯이 그동안 한국 축구는 지도자를 선임할 때 현대 축구 트렌드에 대한 연구나 전술적 역량 검증보다, 과거 선수 시절의 이름값과 명성에 의존하는 구태의연한 방식을 반복해 왔다. 스타 플레이어 출신이라는 후광이 현대 축구의 정교한 전술적 해킹 능력을 보장해주지 않는다는 것을 매번 확인하고도 소 잃고 외양간조차 고치지 않는다. 제대로 된 지도자를 판별할 안목조차 보여주지 못하는 전력강화위원회 시스템부터가 개혁 대상이다.  물리적 시간이 부족하다면, 지금 당장이라도 엄격한 경기력 리뷰를 통해 전술 코칭스태프를 보강하거나 기술적 안전장치라도 수혈해야 한다. 성인 대표팀의 어수선한 틈을 타 아무런 피드백 없이 방치해온 아시안게임 대표팀의 항로를 지금이라도 점검하길 바란다. 대참사가 눈앞에 닥친 뒤에 책임을 물어봐야 그때는 이미 한국 축구의 미래가 증발한 뒤일 것이다."

한국 기업(국내 거래소)이 직접 당한 암호화폐 해킹 사건만 정부순으로 정리했습니다. (Grok)  박근혜 정부 (~2017년 3월) 2017년 4월: Yapizon (Youbit) 1차 해킹 피해: 약 55억 원 (4,000 BTC). 북한 연계 의심. (그록이 이 사건을 왜 박근혜 정부로... 하는거지 이때쯤 아마 탄핵쯤이고 문재인이가 영향력 미친거 아닌가?)  문재인 정부 (2017년 5월 ~ 2022년 5월) 이 기간에 국내 거래소 대형 해킹이 집중됐습니다.2017년 12월: Youbit 2차 해킹 → 파산 피해: 총 자산의 17%. 두 번째 공격으로 거래소 폐쇄. 2018년 4월: Coinrail 해킹 피해: 약 400억 원. 2018년 6월: Bithumb 해킹 피해: 약 350억 원. 2019년 3월: Bithumb 추가 해킹 피해: 약 2,000만 달러 (내부자 의심). 2019년 11월: Upbit 해킹 피해: 34만 2천 ETH (당시 약 580억 원). 북한 라자루스 그룹으로 공식 확인.  윤석열 정부 (2022년 5월 ~ 2025년 6월)2025년 11월: Upbit 2차 해킹 피해: 약 400~450억 원 (Solana 기반 자산). 다시 북한 연계 의심.  이재명 정부 (2025년 6월 ~ 현재)현재까지 대형 해킹 사고 없음 (2026년 2월 Bithumb 오지급 사고는 해킹이 아닌 전산 오류입니다.)  요약 문재인 정부 때 국내 거래소 해킹이 가장 많이 발생했습니다. 대부분 북한 라자루스 그룹이 용의자로 지목됐습니다.  신기하지? 문재인 정부때 집중이 됐어. 해킹을 하라고 문열어둔게 아닐까 싶을 정도로 의심  이재명 정부가 없는 이유는  1. 문재인, 윤석열 정부로 거치면서 많은 보안책등이 생겼다. 2. 아직은 운이 좋다. 3. 아직 기간이 짧다. 정도라는데  미국이 지켜보고 있어서 그런거 아니야?  리셋 되면 문제인 정부도 다 봐야 된다고 생각함.

$PLTR Laser weapons aren't science fiction anymore - and they are flipping the math on defense stocks Why Palantir and Elbit are top plays in the booming counterdrone market  🦾💥🔮  Jun 29, 202 The economics of modern air defense are broken. Counterdrone systems are one of the fastest-growing segments in defense. The reason is a simple imbalance: Cheap drones are overwhelming their expensive interceptors. Defenders facing saturation attacks are burning through million-dollar inventory, and the math compounds quickly.  Directed-energy weapons (DEW) - primarily high-energy lasers and high-power microwave systems - flip this equation. DEW are now moving into procurement, shipboard integration and operational air-defense networks.  The Congressional Research Service estimated that shipboard, solid-state lasers can fire at a marginal cost between $1 and $10 per shot. For example, in U.S. Navy testing, the destroyer USS Preble used Lockheed Martin's (LMT) Helios laser weapon to down four drones in at-sea testing.  Israel, more than any other country, has moved furthest toward operational deployment: the Iron Beam, with Elbit Systems (IL:ESLT) (ESLT) supplying the high-power laser source, was delivered to the IDF in December 2025 for integration into Israel's layered air-defense network, making Israel the first country to field what its defense ministry described as an operational high-power laser interception system.  The science of using focused energy to destroy targets has been explored for decades. What changed is the enabling infrastructure around it. Power generation, storage, beam control and thermal management have matured to a point where directed energy is no longer purely a laboratory proposition. These systems direct a laser to open fire, hold that energy on a target long enough to destroy it, and then continue to attack.  Estimates of the potential market for DEW vary widely, as they often do in emerging defense categories, but most point in the same direction: rapid growth from a still-small base.  Consulting firm Imarc, for example, put the global DEW market, which covers laser and microwave systems across all missions, at roughly $10 billion in 2025 and projected $35.3 billion by 2034, a compound annual growth rate of just over 15%.  Meanwhile, Precedence Research estimated the global counterdrone market, which spans every effector type used against drones, not just lasers, at $2.1 billion in 2025 and projected it to reach $19.1 billion by 2035 - a 25.8% compound annual growth rate from 2026 to 2035.  AI and sensors: the overlooked layers  Lasers get headlines. But the AI-powered detection and targeting layer is what determines whether it actually works. A high-energy laser that cannot precisely track a target moving at speed, or that cannot prioritize among dozens of incoming threats simultaneously, is just an extremely expensive light source.  This creates an investment angle that most coverage misses. The architectures required to make directed-energy systems operational depend on sensor fusion - the real-time integration of radar, electro-optical and infrared data - combined with AI-driven threat classification and engagement prioritization. Against drone swarms in particular, this computational layer is the key.  Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Anduril Industries formed a consortium in December 2024 that brings together Palantir's Maven targeting and data-fusion software with Anduril's Lattice autonomy platform, which connects sensors and effectors across air, land, sea, space and cyber domains into a single operational picture.  In 2025, the U.S. Army selected Lattice as the fire-control platform for the Integrated Battle Command System Maneuver program, a counterdrone command-and-control layer meant to fuse sensor data, automate fire control and compress the detection-to-defeat timeline. Teledyne Technologies (TDY), through its FLIR Defense division, represents another less-visible node. The company's Cerberus XL counterdrone system - described by Teledyne as battle-tested in Ukraine - integrates long-range 3D radar, thermal and optical imaging, and RF detection in a platform capable of tracking up to 500 targets simultaneously. It is designed to be effector-agnostic, meaning it can feed targeting data to kinetic, electronic or directed-energy weapons. That hardware-agnostic approach positions Teledyne as a beneficiary regardless of which terminal effector a given military selects.  Real constraints  Directed-energy weapons carry genuine operational limitations. High-energy lasers are degraded by atmospheric conditions - rain, fog, sand and smoke all scatter and absorb the beam, reducing effective range and time on target. High-power microwave systems are generally less vulnerable to rain, fog or smoke than lasers, but still depend on line of sight, range, beam control and the surrounding electromagnetic environment. Thermal management - keeping the weapon cool enough to fire repeatedly - remains an engineering constraint, particularly on mobile platforms.  Range is another problem. Israel's Iron Beam, for example, is designed to engage targets at roughly 10 kilometers (6.2 miles). That's effective for short-range threats, including drones, rockets and mortars, but it's not a substitute for defense against longer-range threats.  Which countries are actually deploying these weapons? Israel remains the clearest example of a country moving a high-power laser air-defense system into operational service. The U.K. and U.S. are close behind in testing and naval integration, but Israel has crossed the deployment threshold first. The December 2025 delivery of Iron Beam to the Israeli Air Force followed confirmed combat use of a scaled-down precursor system in October 2024, when Israeli laser systems intercepted approximately 40 Hezbollah UAVs. The urgency behind Israel's deployment timeline is inseparable from its operational context: Sustained, high-volume aerial attacks across multiple fronts have created exactly the cost-asymmetry problem that DEW solve.  The United States is not technologically behind - the Pentagon has multiple programs across all service branches - but it has been slower to deploy at scale. The reasons are structural. U.S. procurement cycles are longer and more layered than those in countries operating under active threat.  Russia and Ukraine have both deployed non-kinetic counterdrone systems at scale, especially in electronic warfare, while high-energy laser interception remains a separate and less mature category. The conflict has functioned as an accelerated stress test, compressing years of operational learning into months and validating or refuting assumptions about real-world system performance. That iterative pressure is what drives deployment timelines forward.  China's trajectory is harder to read precisely, but the pattern is one of internal development and rapid adaptation rather than reliance on external technology. Chinese defense publications and procurement activity indicate a multilayered approach that combines radar, electro-optical sensors, electronic warfare, and AI-driven interception - structurally similar to the Western model but developed independently.  The investment landscape  Leading U.S. defense contractors - Lockheed Martin, RTX (RTX) and Northrop Grumman (NOC) - hold established positions across high-end DEW development. But the investment story is more about how beams, sensors, fire control or software are integrated into deployable systems.  Elbit Systems, which supplies high-power laser systems for Israel's Iron Beam, is the closest thing to a pure-play DEW beneficiary among publicly traded defense firms. The company's backlog reached $28.1 billion at the end of 2025, up from $22.6 billion a year earlier, while its Iron Beam role is backed by a contract worth roughly $200 million from the Israeli Ministry of Defense. Lockheed Martin is also tied to Iron Beam, as a development partner for potential U.S. applications, a separate role from Elbit's supply of the laser source itself.  Stock investors should pay attention to the software and sensor layer. Palantir is benefiting from the broader defense shift toward AI-enabled targeting, data-fusion and battlefield-decision software. Teledyne, often categorized as an industrial-sensing company, is embedded in the counterdrone detection chain through systems already operational in active theaters.  The investment parallel worth considering is the shift that cloud computing brought to enterprise IT - where the cost and scalability advantages of a new delivery model eventually restructured the entire industry, benefiting infrastructure, software and integration players. DEW could similarly disrupt the defense sector. The winners will not be just those who generate the laser beam, but those who control the energy behind it and the intelligence that decides when to fire. By Jurica Dujmovic 🇺🇸🛡️🦾💥🔮⚡️ morningstar.com/news/marketw…

$AVAV Q4 2026 earnings: Record Q4 Finish Masks Underlying Margin and Segment Divergences  AeroVironment closed a transformational FY26 with a massive Q4, generating $641.6 million in revenue and driving 31% organic YoY growth. The Q3 miss caused by the SCAR program termination is in the rear-view mirror operationally, with Adjusted EBITDA hitting a record $140.1 million (22% margin). However, beneath the headline beat, the financial profile is shifting dramatically. While Autonomous Systems surged 49% pro forma YoY, the Space, Cyber & Directed Energy (SCDE) segment shrank 8%. Furthermore, a collapse in service margins and ballooning unbilled receivables hint at integration growing pains. FY27 guidance projects healthy 10% revenue growth, but heavy capital expenditures and depreciation will hold Adjusted EPS essentially flat.  Full article with charts - link in bio  🐂 𝐁𝐮𝐥𝐥 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞 • 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐨𝐧𝐨𝐦𝐨𝐮𝐬 𝐒𝐲𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐬 𝐇𝐲𝐩𝐞𝐫-𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐰𝐭𝐡 — The core AxS segment is accelerating rapidly, with Precision Strike & Defensive Systems revenue soaring 80% YoY on a pro forma basis in Q4. Product-level gross margins rebounded sharply to 44%. • 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐁𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐥𝐨𝐠 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲 — Funded backlog reached a record $1.2 billion, completely replacing the $1.5 billion unfunded backlog wipeout from the Q3 SCAR termination. Management claims 69% visibility into the midpoint of FY27 revenue guidance.  🐻 𝐁𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞 • 𝐒𝐂𝐃𝐄 𝐒𝐞𝐠𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 — Despite management's narrative regarding the transformational success of the BlueHalo acquisition, the SCDE segment pro forma revenue actually declined 8% YoY in Q4, dragged down by a 26% drop in Cyber & Mission Solutions. • 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐚𝐩𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐃𝐞𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 — Unbilled receivables nearly doubled YoY to $570.4 million from $290.0 million. This structural shift toward overtime revenue recognition is severely pressuring operating cash flow, which was negative $78.4 million for the year.  ⚖️ 𝐕𝐞𝐫𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐭: ⚪ Neutral. The top-line scale and product margin recovery in Q4 are highly impressive, validating the core Autonomous Systems demand. However, the stalling SCDE segment, collapsing service margins, and cash flow burn require close monitoring. The stock needs flawless execution to support a valuation expecting continuous hyper-growth.  𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬  🟢 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐄𝐱𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐀𝐱𝐒 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐰𝐭𝐡 Accelerating. The Autonomous Systems (AxS) segment delivered a massive $492.4 million in Q4, representing 49% pro forma YoY growth. The standout was the Precision Strike & Defensive Systems unit, which surged 80% YoY to $333 million. This reflects scaling production for Switchblade variants and robust international demand via massive new IDIQ vehicles.  🟢 𝐆𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐟𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐭𝐬 𝐅𝐮𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐔𝐧𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐃𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝 Stable. The macro backdrop remains a major tailwind. Management previously cited active conflicts involving Ukraine and Iran as driving 'unprecedented' demand for one-way attack drones (Switchblade, Red Dragon) and counter-UAS systems (Titan, LO-CUST). Foreign Military Sales (FMS) IDIQ vehicles are translating international panic into tangible, high-margin product orders.  🟢 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐮𝐟𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐞 Reversing. After quarters of gross margin compression due to mix shifts and early-stage BlueHalo programs, Q4 Adjusted Product Gross Margin spiked back to 44% (from 32% in Q3). This proves the company's thesis that scaling production at new facilities (like the Utah factory) can successfully dilute fixed costs on core hardware.  🔴 𝐒𝐂𝐃𝐄 𝐒𝐞𝐠𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐃𝐫𝐚𝐠 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐭𝐬 '𝐀𝐥𝐥-𝐃𝐨𝐦𝐚𝐢𝐧' 𝐒𝐲𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐠𝐲 𝐍𝐚𝐫𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 [NEW] Decelerating. Management frequently highlights the BlueHalo acquisition as a masterstroke positioning the company for multi-domain dominance. Yet, Q4 data explicitly contradicts this unblemished narrative: the SCDE segment's pro forma revenue fell 8% YoY to $150 million. The Cyber & Mission Solutions sub-segment was the main culprit, plunging 26% YoY, highlighting the lumpiness and potential discontinuation of legacy cyber programs.  🔴🔴 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐚𝐩𝐬𝐞 [NEW] Decelerating. A severe red flag emerged in the profitability of contract services. Adjusted Service Gross Margin plummeted to a mere 2% in Q4, down from 17% in Q3 and 13% in Q1. While product mix shielded the aggregate margin, the fact that a $142.6 million revenue stream is generating virtually zero gross profit indicates severe cost overruns or unfavorable fixed-price service contracts tied to the BlueHalo integration.  🔴 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐚𝐩𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐃𝐫𝐚𝐢𝐧 𝐯𝐢𝐚 𝐔𝐧𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐢𝐯𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞𝐬 Stable. The balance sheet reflects a growing strain on cash conversion. Unbilled receivables and retentions spiked to $570.4 million, up 96% from $290.0 million a year ago. This is driving persistently negative operating cash flows (-$78.4 million for FY26). The shift toward overtime revenue recognition, inherited from BlueHalo, creates a permanent drag on cash velocity compared to legacy AeroVironment.  🟢 𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐅𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐬: 𝐋𝐎-𝐂𝐔𝐒𝐓 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐏𝟓𝟓𝟎 Product innovation remains central to the long-term thesis. The P550 UAS, designed for the Army's Long Range Reconnaissance (LRR) program, and the LOCUST directed energy laser weapon are positioned to unseat legacy incumbents. Management's strategy of self-funding R&D to deliver mature, commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) technology is winning against traditional cost-plus defense primes.  𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐊𝐏𝐈𝐬  𝐎𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐰 (𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟔): -$78.4 million Cash generation completely deteriorated from a mere -$1.3 million outflow in FY25 to -$78.4 million in FY26. While net income was skewed by non-cash impairments, the underlying culprit was a massive $158.9 million working capital absorption into unbilled receivables and $111.6 million into inventory build-ups. The company is funding its scale-up entirely from the balance sheet.  𝐔𝐧𝐟𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐝 𝐁𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐥𝐨𝐠: $1.46 billion Down dramatically from nearly $3.0 billion earlier in the year. The Q4 roll-forward explicitly shows a $1.49 billion reduction directly attributed to the SCAR termination, validating that the massive $1.5 billion "future potential" of that program has been entirely wiped from the books. The remaining backlog is healthier, but the optics of the pipeline are smaller.  𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞  𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $2.125 - $2.225 billion Decelerating. The midpoint of $2.175 billion implies roughly 10% YoY growth. This is a significant deceleration from the 26% organic growth achieved in FY26. Guidance incorporates a 45% H1 / 55% H2 weighting, suggesting heavy reliance on Q3/Q4 execution once again.  𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐀𝐝𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐄𝐁𝐈𝐓𝐃𝐀: $305 - $325 million Stable. The midpoint of $315 million implies a ~10% YoY increase, maintaining the FY26 Adjusted EBITDA margin at approximately 14.5%. This indicates that while product margins improved in Q4, overall profitability will remain weighed down by lower-margin service revenues and elevated IRAD (guided at 7-9% of revenue).  𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐀𝐝𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐄𝐏𝐒 (𝐃𝐢𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐞𝐝): $3.02 - $3.34 Reversing. The midpoint of $3.18 implies a ~4% YoY decline compared to the $3.31 achieved in FY26. This disconnect between EBITDA growth and EPS contraction is explicitly driven by higher depreciation expenses due to scaling manufacturing facilities, integration-related CapEx (guided at 12-14% of revenue), and a higher outstanding share count following equity raises.  𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐐𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬  𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐚𝐩𝐬𝐞 Adjusted Service Gross Margin dropped to 2% in Q4. What specific programs drove this compression, and are these fixed-price contracts structurally underwater, or is this a one-time integration noise?  𝐒𝐂𝐃𝐄 𝐒𝐞𝐠𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 Pro forma revenue for Cyber & Mission Solutions fell 26% YoY in Q4. Is this due to delayed task orders, or have certain legacy BlueHalo programs been discontinued or lost to competitors?  𝐏𝐚𝐭𝐡 𝐭𝐨 𝐅𝐫𝐞𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐰 With capital expenditures forecasted at 12-14% of revenue in FY27 and unbilled receivables continuing to drain working capital, when does management expect the combined entity to generate positive, sustainable free cash flow?  𝐒𝐂𝐀𝐑 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐫𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐄𝐱𝐞𝐜𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 Management previously indicated a pivot to commercializing the Badger/SCAR technology. What specific milestones are embedded in the FY27 guidance for this product line, and is there any revenue assumed?

Defense finally gets an edge the attacker can't buy: the good guys get access to the best model, the attacker doesn't.

MENENE RANSOMWARE?  Ransomware wani nau'in malware (virus mai cutarwa) ne da ke shiga kwamfuta ko waya, sannan ya kulle (encrypt) dukkan fayiloli ko tsarin na'urar,  Bayan haka, masu harin suna buƙatar a biya su kuɗi (ransom) domin a sake buɗe fayilolin.  A sauƙaƙe: Ransomware = Virus da ke kulle bayananka, sannan ya nemi kuɗin fansa kafin ya sake buɗe su.  Yaya Ransomware ke aiki? Mutum ya danna link ko ya buɗe email mai cutarwa. Virus ɗin ya shiga na'urar. Ya fara encrypting dukkan fayiloli (Photos, Videos, Documents, da sauransu).  Bayan ya gama, sai ya nuna saƙo kamar: "An kulle duk bayananka. Ka biya $500 cikin Bitcoin domin ka dawo da su."  Ta yaya ake kamuwa da Ransomware? Buɗe email daga wanda ba ka sani ba. Sauke software daga shafukan da ba su da aminci. Amfani da pirated/cracked software. Danna links marasa aminci. Rashin sabunta tsarin kwamfuta (Operating System). Amfani da USB mai ɗauke da virus.  Illolin Ransomware Rashin samun damar amfani da fayiloli. Asarar muhimman bayanai. Asarar kuɗi idan aka biya fansa. Kasuwanci na iya tsayawa gaba ɗaya. Ana iya satar bayanan sirri.  Yadda za ka kare kanka Kada ka buɗe email ko attachment daga wanda ba ka sani ba. Ka yi amfani da antivirus mai kyau kuma ka riƙa sabunta shi. Ka riƙa sabunta Windows ko tsarin wayarka. Ka guji amfani da cracked software. Ka yi backup na bayananka akai-akai (external drive ko cloud). Ka yi amfani da kalmomin sirri masu ƙarfi da kuma Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). Idan an kamu da Ransomware fa? Cire haɗin na'urar daga internet nan da nan. Kada ka biya kuɗin fansa, domin babu tabbacin za a dawo da fayilolinka. Tuntuɓi ƙwararrun masu tsaro na yanar gizo (cybersecurity experts). Yi ƙoƙarin dawo da bayanai daga backup idan kana da shi. A binciki na'urar kafin a sake amfani da ita.  Takaitawa Ransomware na ɗaya daga cikin manyan barazanar tsaro a duniya. Yana kulle bayananka sannan ya nemi kuɗin fansa. Hanya mafi kyau ta kariya ita ce yin taka-tsantsan, sabunta na'urori, amfani da antivirus, da kuma yin backup akai-akai.

Sale of ZeroDay C2 panel for iOS and Android with claimed zero-click full-chain exploitation  For informational purposes only.  Affected platforms: iOS 13–26.5+ on all chips, Android all versions  The seller positions the tool as a zero-click full-chain exploitation framework for iOS and Android, which supposedly allows infecting a device with a single link without any interaction from the victim.  According to the author's description, the exploitation chain starts with a browser RCE, then proceeds through an escape from the sandbox, obtains kernel read/write, and installs a persistent implant. The seller specifically emphasizes that the tool does not depend on a specific delivery channel: the link can be sent via SMS, email, QR code, WhatsApp, Telegram, or any other mechanism for sharing a URL.  The listings claim support for all iOS builds from iOS 13 to 26.5+ and all versions of Android. The seller also claims that the tool will adapt to all OS updates. The listings are accompanied by video demonstrations.  The claimed set of features is very extensive. The seller lists modules for extracting Keychain, SMS/MMS, WhatsApp ChatStorage.sqlite, Telegram Axolotl.sqlite, iMessage, Signal, contacts, file system, browser history, cookies, saved passwords, notes, calendar, Apple Health, Google Fit, Wi-Fi networks and passwords, SIM/IMSI/IMEI, call logs, Find My iPhone configuration, and a list of installed apps.  A separate section is dedicated to surveillance capabilities: real-time camera feed from the front and main camera, live microphone capture, GPS tracking, GPS timeline dump, screen recorder, clipboard logger, notification listener, and keylogger. According to the seller, the panel also supports remote terminal, multi-device management, real-time dashboard, geofencing, scheduled task automation, reboot-resistant persistence watchdog, C2 tunneling via HTTPS/DNS/ICMP/WebSocket, remote kill switch, and a trace-removal module.  In addition, some of the features are aimed at stealing cryptocurrency assets and banking data.  The description also includes offensive tooling for packaging and delivering payloads: custom exploit builder, payload generator for RCE/reverse shell/bind shell, phishing page customizer, exploit porter for SMS/email/QR/WhatsApp/Telegram, AES-256 and ChaCha20 payload encryption, and AV/AMSI/EDR obfuscation packer.  #dbugs_darkweb

Nissan の従業員情報流出で、最初に見るべき数字は「5月27日〜6月9日」だと思った。  Oracle PeopleSoft のゼロデイが悪用された期間。  だが、調べるほど、それだけでは足りないと分かる。  5月27日〜6月9日というのは、あくまで観測されたゼロデイ悪用期間だ。  問題は、その前から PeopleSoft の周辺が狙われ続けていたことにある。  今回の攻撃では、/PSEMHUB/ と /PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector が出てくる。  そして /PSIGW/HttpListeningConnector は、少なくとも2013年の PeopleSoft exploit chain にも登場していた。  つまりこれは、突然現れた一発のゼロデイではない。  長年狙われてきた業務基盤の継ぎ目に、今回はゼロデイ、既知経路、SSRF、RCE、横展開、データ窃取が重なった話だ。  ゼロデイという言葉の意味も重い。  既知の脆弱性を放置した、という単純な話ではない。  ベンダーが正式に修正策を出す前に、攻撃者が先に悪用していた。  この段階で、利用企業にできることはかなり限られる。  パッチはまだない。 IOC もまだない。 「すぐ当てればよかった」では済まない。  そして本当に厳しいのはここからだ。  Oracle がパッチや緩和策を出せば、ベンダーとしての対応は一区切りになる。  しかし顧客企業側では、そこから説明責任が始まる。  誰の情報が抜かれたのか。 給与、税務、銀行口座、扶養家族情報は含まれるのか。 従業員通知、当局対応、調査費用、信用毀損、訴訟リスクを誰が負うのか。  しかも、契約上 Oracle に全額賠償を求めるのは簡単ではない。  エンタープライズソフトウェア契約では、損害賠償の範囲や上限が限定されることが多い。  「Oracle のゼロデイだから Oracle が全部補償してくれる」とは考えにくい。  結局、被害を受けた企業が、調査も、通知も、説明も、信用回復も背負う。  ---  だから今回見るべきなのは、Nissan という個社名だけではない。  人事。 給与。 税務。 銀行口座。 扶養家族。 受益者情報。  こうした従業員の人生情報を抱えた業務基盤そのものが、攻撃対象になっている。  ERP を使っているすべての企業が、いま確認すべきことがある。  自社の業務基盤はどこに、何を持っているか。 何か起きたとき、契約上誰が損を負うか。  Nissan は入口に過ぎない。  bleepingcomputer.com/news/se… #Nissan #Oracle #PeopleSoft #ZeroDay #サイバーセキュリティ #情報漏えい #個人情報保護 #基幹システム #契約リスク
#Nissan discloses employee #databreach linked to Oracle PeopleSoft #zeroday cyber attacks:  bleepingcomputer.com/news/se…

Google Maps side hustle that can get you to $10K/month in less than 6 months.  It requires zero followers. Zero ad spend. Zero inventory.  No coding. Not even a single line of HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.  Just GoHighLevel — a drag-and-drop platform with pre-built templates for every local business niche + Google Maps.  Point. Click. Customize. Publish. 20 minutes. Done.  Here's how YOU can copy this without touching a single line of code:  The biggest lie in the online business world is that you need to be "technical" to make money.  You don't.  You don't need to code. You don't need to design from scratch. You don't need to understand hosting, DNS, or any of that nonsense.  GoHighLevel has already built everything for you.  Professional website templates for plumbers, roofers, dentists, cleaners, contractors, mechanics — every local business type you can think of.  Drag-and-drop editor. Pre-made sections. Built-in contact forms.  Integrated booking calendars. AI chatbots ready to deploy. Voice agents that answer phone calls. Review automation that sends Google review requests. SMS sequences that follow up with leads.  All in one platform. All with zero coding.  You literally pick a template, swap in the business owner's logo and name, adjust the colors, and publish.  If you can use Canva, you can do this.  Here's the exact breakdown:  STEP 1: FIND PROSPECTS ON GOOGLE MAPS  Search "plumber [city]" or "roofer [city]" or "dentist [city]."  Look for businesses with 20+ reviews but NO website (or a broken one). These are active, revenue-generating businesses that are invisible online.  In 30 minutes: 15-20 qualified prospects.  In one afternoon: 100.  Add them to a spreadsheet. This is your goldmine.  STEP 2: BUILD THEIR WEBSITE IN GOHIGHLEVEL  Log into GoHighLevel. Go to Sites → Website.  Choose a template:  • City Plumber for plumbing businesses • Roofing Pro for roofers • Medical/Dental for dentists and chiropractors • Cleaning Services for residential/commercial cleaners • Contractor Builder for general contractors  These aren't basic templates. They're conversion-optimized, mobile-responsive, professionally designed sites built specifically for local service businesses.  Each template includes:  → Prominent phone number and call-to-action in the header → Hero section that immediately communicates what the business does → Trust signals (review counts, years in business, license numbers) → Clear service descriptions → Quote request forms → Appointment booking calendars → Mobile optimization (60%+ of local searches happen on phones)  Customization takes 10-20 minutes:  • Replace the template business name with your prospect's actual name • Update phone number, email, and address • Upload their logo (find it on their Google Maps listing or social media) • Adjust colors to match their brand • Update services to match what they actually offer • That's it. No coding. No design skills. No technical knowledge.  If you can copy and paste text and upload an image, you can build these sites.  STEP 3: RECORD A 3-MINUTE LOOM VIDEO  Show their broken website (or lack of one). Show what you built for them in GoHighLevel.  "Hey [Name], I was looking for [service] in [city] and found your business. I noticed you have great reviews but [no website / a broken website]. So I went ahead and built you a quick lead generation site. It has your logo, your services, and captures customer info automatically. I'd love your feedback."  Send it. Follow up in 48 hours.  STEP 4: CLOSE WITH THE YES OFFER  "I charge $297 per month for the full system. That includes: ✦ Your professional website ✦ AI chatbot that answers questions and books appointments 24/7 ✦ AI voice agent that answers missed calls ✦ Review automation that brings in 5-star Google ratings ✦ SMS follow-up sequences for leads  Plus a one-time $500 setup fee to customize everything to your brand. No contract. Cancel anytime. If you don't get at least one new lead in 30 days, I'll refund your first month."  Then ask: "How much is one new customer worth to you?"  Let them do the math. One $1,000 customer pays for 3+ months of your service.  STEP 5: DELIVER THE STICKY SYSTEM IN GOHIGHLEVEL  This is where GoHighLevel becomes your secret weapon.  Everything is built-in. No third-party plugins. No duct-taping tools together.  The AI Chatbot: → Lives on the website → Answers visitor questions 24/7 → Collects name, phone, email → Books appointments directly on the business owner's calendar → All conversations logged as leads in the CRM  The AI Voice Agent: → Answers missed phone calls → Has natural conversations with callers → Books appointments automatically → Sends conversation transcripts to the business owner's mobile app → The client pays for usage on their own credit card — zero cost to you  The Review Automation: → Sends SMS review requests after completed jobs → Includes direct link to Google review page → Auto-responds to positive reviews → Tracks review count and rating improvements → All visible in the mobile app  The SMS Follow-Up Sequences: → Immediate confirmation after form submission → Next-day check-in for quote requests → Value-add tips 48 hours later → Appointment reminders → Post-service review requests  The Mobile App: → Every lead notification goes straight to the client's phone → Every appointment booking pops up instantly → Every new review triggers a notification → The client sees their business growing in real-time → They associate every win with YOUR service  All of this is included in GoHighLevel. One platform. One login. One monthly subscription.  You don't build any of it from scratch. You configure it. You customize it.  You turn it on.  The client pays you $297/month to manage a system that runs itself.  The math:  1 client × $297 = $3,564/year 5 clients × $297 = $17,820/year 10 clients × $297 = $35,640/year 20 clients × $297 = $71,280/year 50 clients × $297 = $178,200/year  GoHighLevel costs YOU $297/month for the Agency Unlimited plan.  One client and you break even.  Two clients and you're profitable.  Everything beyond that is nearly pure profit.  You can even start with their 14 day free trial.  $0  Time investment for 10 clients: ~21 hours/month.  That's $141/hour. And it gets faster with every client as your templates improve.  The 48-hour launch:  Day 1: Sign up for GoHighLevel's 14-day free trial. Build one demo site. Find 20 prospects. Write your scripts.  Day 2: Build 3 pre-built sites. Record 3 Loom videos. Send outreach. Follow up.  By day 3, you've accomplished more than most people do in a month of "thinking about it."  Month 1: 1-2 clients. Learn GoHighLevel. Refine your pitch. Month 2: 3-5 clients. Templates get faster. Referrals start. Month 3: 5-8 clients. Case studies built. Confidence high. Time to scale.  Realistic for 5-10 hours per week.  The AI tools in GoHighLevel do the heavy lifting. The templates do the design work. The automation handles the ongoing work.  Your job is simple:  → Find businesses on Google Maps → Build their site using a template → Send a Loom video → Configure the built-in AI tools → Collect $297/month  No coding. No design degree. No technical background needed. If you can use a computer, you can do this.  The businesses are out there. They have the money. They have the need. They just don't have the system.  GoHighLevel gives you the system. You just need to show up and execute.  If you want the complete playbook with all of the outreach scripts, and the step-by-step system to your first 10k month and beyond:  Comment "WANT" below and I'll send it over to you via DM's

THIS is what happens when you pray the rosary (according to Padre Pio)   From Sister Clara Wren, 25 June 2026, @thehealingkitchen channel ———————————————-  ➡️ "Sister Clara remembered Padre Pio’s teaching that the rosary is a weapon that works whether feelings are present or absent." ———————————————- Padre Pio called the rosary "the weapon" — and he prayed up to 40 rosaries every single day until his death. But what actually happens the moment you pick up those beads? According to this saint who bore the wounds of Christ for 50 years, the rosary does something most of us have never been told. It releases souls from Purgatory. It binds demons. It puts your body into a measurable state of healing. And it invites the Blessed Mother to kneel beside you, handing every single bead to the Father as intercession.  In this video, I share what Padre Pio taught about the rosary — the physical effects, the spiritual warfare, the impossible healings, and the 54-day novena that changed hundreds of lives. Whether you've prayed the rosary for years or never picked one up, this will transform the way you see those beads.  🕊️ What Padre Pio said happens every time you say a Hail Mary:  🕊️ The medical study that proved the rosary heals your body   🕊️ Why the enemy fights your rosary (and what that means)   🕊️ The 54-day novena Padre Pio endorsed above all else   🕊️ How the rosary releases souls from Purgatory — every single bead  Timestamps:   0:00 – What Padre Pio called the rosary   2:14 – The moment Mary kneels beside you   5:41 – Every bead releases a soul from Purgatory   8:29 – The rosary heals your body (medical proof)   11:52 – What happens when your mind wanders   15:06 – The 54-day novena that breaks the impossible   19:33 – What happens in the unseen when you pray   23:47 – Why resistance means it's working  If Padre Pio is right — and I believe he is — then the rosary is the one thing we can't afford to neglect. This is the weapon. This is the telescope through which we see eternity. Pick it up. Pray it. Let Mary do the rest. 🙏  ➡️ DETAILED SUMMARY below video link   ➡️ Watch 23:21 min video 👇 👇 👇  youtu.be/fiuNYLqht54 ———————————————-  Reposted from @infidel_cracka & @thehealingkitchenchannel  DETAILED SUMMARY THIS is what happens when you pray the rosary (according to Padre Pio)  From Sister Clara Wren, 25 June 2026, @thehealingkitchen channel  0:00 Discovering the True Power of the Rosary Through Padre Pio   Sister Clara Wren begins by describing a familiar moment many Catholics experience. You pick up a rosary and part of you wonders if you are simply going through motions. You say the words and hope something on the other side is listening. She felt this way for years. She prayed the rosary because she was told it mattered and because it was tradition. Good Catholics do it, yet she did not truly know what was happening.  Everything changed when she began reading what Padre Pio taught about the rosary. Once she understood his words, she could never view the beads the same way again. She now shares what this saint, who bore the wounds of Christ on his own body for fifty years, revealed about the exact moment someone begins to pray those beads. The rosary is not passive or merely symbolic. Something real begins the instant your fingers touch the first bead.  Padre Pio called the rosary the weapon. He did not mean a weapon in the modern sense of something that harms or destroys. He meant it the way a shepherd means a staff or a surgeon means a scalpel. It is a precise tool that accomplishes exactly what it was designed to do. He taught that without the rosary we are defenseless. This statement sounds dramatic, yet he did not use such language lightly.   He lived in a reality where the spiritual battle felt as tangible as any physical conflict. He saw demons. He fought them directly. He bled from wounds that had no medical explanation. Over and over he insisted that the rosary was the one thing the enemy could not withstand.  In a letter to a woman named Gratziela who suffered tormenting evil thoughts, Padre Pio gave clear instruction. Hold the rosary during temptation because the demons cannot bear the touch of a Hail Mary spoken through fingers that love Mary. The fingers do not need to be perfect or sinless. They only need to love Mary. That is sufficient. Years later her daughter testified that the tormenting thoughts stopped within three days. Gratziela held the beads and said the words even when she felt nothing happening. Something was happening nonetheless. Padre Pio knew it from the beginning, and eventually she knew it too.  2:22 The moment Mary kneels beside you   The first concrete action occurs the moment you make the sign of the cross and touch the crucifix. You are no longer alone. Padre Pio told a seminarian in 1965 that when someone prays the rosary the Blessed Mother kneels beside that person. This is not an abstract spiritual idea. She kneels right there. Together the person and Mary hand each bead to the Father as intercession. Every single bead becomes part of this joint offering.  You and Mary stand side by side holding up these prayers like offerings. This image transforms the entire experience. The rosary is no longer an effort to capture God’s attention. It becomes participation in something that is already taking place. Mary is already praying. She is already interceding. The rosary allows the person to step into her prayer. It lets her teach how to pray the way she prayed at the Annunciation, at the foot of the Cross, and while waiting in the Upper Room for the Holy Spirit.  The rosary is therefore not the individual’s work alone. It is her work, and the person receives the grace to participate in it. This understanding removes the pressure of performing perfectly and replaces it with the joy of joining an ongoing heavenly conversation.  5:43 Every bead releases a soul from Purgatory   Padre Pio taught something even more astonishing about each individual bead. Every single Hail Mary releases a soul from Purgatory. He did not say the prayer might help or could possibly assist. He stated that it does release a soul. His spiritual director documented this teaching. Padre Pio himself prayed up to forty rosaries every day. That equals two hundred mysteries and three thousand Hail Marys daily. Each one of those prayers, he said, pulled a soul out of suffering and brought that soul into the presence of God.  Consider what this means in practice. One complete rosary contains fifty-three Hail Marys when the opening prayers are counted. That single rosary therefore releases fifty-three souls. These are people the person praying has never met. They lived, they died, and they waited in purification. Because someone chose to pick up the beads that day, they are released. Fatigue, distraction, or wandering thoughts do not cancel this effect. If Padre Pio’s teaching is true, and he staked his entire reputation on its truth, then no rosary is ever wasted.  The teaching extends beyond help for souls in Purgatory. Padre Pio also described what the rosary accomplishes inside the person praying right now, in body, mind, and heart.  8:34 The rosary heals your body (medical proof)   In this state the nervous system registers complete safety. Heart rate stabilizes. Blood pressure drops. Stress hormones decrease. The parasympathetic nervous system, the division responsible for healing and restoration, activates fully. These changes are measurable and repeatable. They are not mystical claims but observable physiological responses. The rosary, prayed at the rhythm the Church has used for centuries, places the body into a verifiable state of healing.  Padre Pio taught the same reality long before any modern study existed. He told people the rosary heals. Hundreds of pilgrims who visited San Giovanni Rotondo reported spontaneous cures after praying the rosary in his presence. Cancers disappeared. Paralysis reversed. Addictions broke. These healings occurred because the rosary accomplishes what it was created to do.  11:58 What happens when your mind wanders   Many people worry that their rosary does not count if their mind wanders, if they lose their place, or if they fall asleep. Padre Pio’s answer is direct. It still counts. The rosary is not measured by performance or perfect focus. It is measured by the simple yes of picking up the beads and beginning. Even when the person stumbles through every word or remains distracted the entire time, Mary remains present. She continues kneeling beside the person and continues handing each bead to the Father.  Padre Pio compared neglecting the rosary to refusing a mother’s outstretched hand during a storm. He never demanded perfect prayer. He only asked that the person not refuse her hand. The instruction is simple: pick it up and start. Mary will do the rest.  Padre Pio described the rosary as the telescope through which we see eternity. He wrote these words in a 1962 letter. A telescope does not create new objects. It reveals what already exists but lies beyond natural sight. When the rosary is prayed, the person sees what is already true. Heaven intersects with earth. Mary’s intercession becomes visible. God’s mercy flows in ways that do not depend on personal worthiness, eloquence, or concentration. Everything rests on the yes spoken when the beads were first taken up.  Sister Clara Wren shares her own experience during a season of deep doubt. She questioned whether her prayers mattered at all. She felt as though she were shouting into emptiness. One night she nearly put her rosary away for good. Instead she prayed a single decade. She felt nothing. No peace, no warmth, no sense of presence. Only words.   She continued the next day and the day after. Around the twelfth day something shifted inside her. Her circumstances remained unchanged, yet the panic grew quieter and the anger softened. She no longer felt alone. A steady, quiet presence had grown through the repeated prayer. She remembered Padre Pio’s teaching that the rosary is a weapon that works whether feelings are present or absent.  15:08 The 54-day novena that breaks the impossible   15:08 The 54-day novena that breaks the impossible Padre Pio endorsed the fifty-four-day rosary novena more strongly than almost any other practice. The novena consists of twenty-seven days of petition for a specific intention followed by twenty-seven days of thanksgiving, even before any answer appears. His confessor recorded numerous cases in which people who completed the full fifty-four days witnessed outcomes that seemed impossible. Terminal diagnoses reversed. Marriages separated for decades were restored. Addictions that had ruined lives for years broke within weeks. No medical treatment or psychological intervention explained these changes. Only the consistent praying of the rosary remained.  Resistance often appears when the rosary begins to take effect. Difficulty starting, frequent interruptions, sudden heaviness, or unexplained distraction are common. Padre Pio taught that such resistance is actually confirmation the prayer is working. The enemy fights whatever threatens him and wastes no energy on what does not matter. When resistance is felt, the proper response is to press in rather than stop. Praying through the difficulty proves the rosary has struck its target.  19:35 What happens in the unseen when you pray   Padre Pio taught that every Hail Mary produces an immediate response in the spiritual realm. Heaven reacts right then. Angels attend. Saints join the prayer. Mary herself lifts it to her Son. Demons cannot remain. The rosary is unbearable to them. Padre Pio once described it as a chain that binds evil spirits. Each bead adds another link. Continued prayer tightens the chain until the demons have no choice but to depart.  This reality matters especially when praying for someone trapped in addiction, despair, or persistent sin. The rosary binds the enemy’s power over that person. The one praying does not need to be specially holy. The power belongs to the rosary itself when it is used as Padre Pio taught.  Padre Pio meditated deeply on the mysteries rather than reciting them mechanically. He lived them. During the sorrowful mysteries he wept, and those nearby could hear his tears. He was not performing. He was entering the Passion itself. He stood with Mary at the foot of the Cross. He witnessed the scourging and the crowning with thorns. He taught that meditating on the mysteries is not merely remembering ancient events. It is actual participation in them. The mysteries exist outside of time. They are eternal. Praying the agony in the garden places the person in the garden. Praying the resurrection places the person at the empty tomb. Mary shows what she saw, felt, and knew in those moments.  Because of this participation the rosary never becomes boring or merely repetitive. It functions as a door. Each time it is prayed, the person walks through that door into a moment capable of profound change.The rosary is not reserved for the elderly, the leisured, or the already holy. Padre Pio gave rosaries to soldiers, to children, and to people who had never prayed before. He once told a young man that if he did not know how to pray he should simply hold the rosary and say the words. Mary would teach him everything else. Understanding every mystery or feeling every prayer is unnecessary. The only requirement is to begin.  Transformation occurs because Mary prays with the person. She supplies the power. Human effort and concentration are not the source. Her presence and intercession are.  A striking story from San Giovanni Rotondo illustrates the point. A desperate woman came to Padre Pio because her son was dying and doctors had given up hope. She asked him to pray. He asked whether she had prayed the rosary for her son. She admitted she had not. Fear and anxiety had kept her from it. Padre Pio instructed her to go home and pray the rosary without stopping until she had completed a full novena. She obeyed. On the ninth day her son woke up. The fever broke. The infection cleared. Doctors described the recovery as inexplicable. Padre Pio showed no surprise. He had witnessed the rosary’s power countless times and spent his life urging others to discover it for themselves.  The final question Sister Clara Wren poses is practical. What would happen if the rosary were prayed every day for the next fifty-four days, even imperfectly? What enemy stronghold might break? What soul might be released from Purgatory? What healing might begin in the heart? Padre Pio described the rosary as the telescope through which eternity becomes visible. If he is correct, every bead accomplishes something unseen that will one day be fully understood. The difficulties of life may partly result from fighting spiritual battles without the weapon that was given.  Padre Pio prayed forty rosaries daily until the day he died. He did so because he knew the results. Souls were released. Demons were bound. Healings were unleashed. Mary knelt beside him and handed every bead to the Father. He wanted others to know the same reality.  The invitation is straightforward. Pray the rosary. Do not wait until readiness, time, or understanding arrives. Pick up the beads and begin. Let Mary do the rest. According to Padre Pio, that is the moment when everything changes. Saying yes to the beads is saying yes to the weapon. And the weapon never fails.  ➡️ Watch 23:21 min video 👇 👇 👇  youtu.be/fiuNYLqht54 ———————————————-  Reposted from @infidel_cracka & @thehealingkitchenchannel

CLASS IS IN SESSION  The chart's real journalistic vulnerability isn't that the numbers are fabricated, they're not, they're from a real Lancet paper and a real CGD estimate.  The vulnerability is that it presents a counterfactual-driven simulation (built on an assumption that 20 years of historical funding-mortality correlation will hold unchanged for five future years with zero substitution effects) as if it carries the same evidentiary weight as a measured outcome.  The spread between the CGD floor (3.8M) and the "deepening cuts" ceiling (22.6M), a nearly sixfold range, is itself the tell that this is model-dependent, not fact-dependent. Spy and Villain by Joy Lilley, is a compelling short story that has touched readers with its poignant blend of suspense, compassion, and quiet heroism, earning praise for its heartfelt portrayal of observation, empathy, and the triumph of kindness over suspicion in a tale infused with gentle Christian themes. This concise yet impactful narrative centers on Mavis, a physically handicapped woman confined to a wheelchair following an unfortunate accident, who spends her days discreetly observing the world from behind her curtains. When she becomes convinced that her neighbor across the road is enduring abuse—marked by troubling signs and the woman's eventual disappearance—Mavis refuses to dismiss her instincts, even as her husband brushes off her concerns. Determined to uncover the truth, Mavis transforms into an unlikely "spy," venturing beyond her home to befriend the vulnerable woman, leading to a dramatic revelation that proves her right and forges a meaningful, supportive friendship amid ongoing distress.  Readers have appreciated the story's emotional warmth and moral depth despite its brevity. An Amazon reviewer shared, “A charming short story... the lady in the wheelchair who spends much of her time looking at others through her window,” highlighting its engaging premise. Another noted the Christian undertones that guide the protagonist's actions toward redemption and connection, with one calling it “a tale of a spy... proved to be right. And becomes a friend of the abused woman.” Promotional features on blogs like Book Marketing Global Network and Something or Other Publishing emphasize its dramatic turn and themes of suspicion turning to solidarity, while its availability on Kindle Unlimited has helped it reach fans of inspirational short fiction. With a solid 3.6-star average on Amazon from early readers, it stands out for its relatable character and uplifting resolution.  @joygerken zurl.co/crJVC zurl.co/FEDfs zurl.co/Nnnz2 zurl.co/TzU9o zurl.co/b44O9 zurl.co/gMZzY    Joy M. Lilley, a British author and retired registered nurse (real name Joy Gerken), has built a devoted following with her empathetic, character-focused stories that draw from life's challenges and triumphs. Residing in Kent—the garden of England—and a proud grandmother, her nursing background at places like the Royal Victoria Hospital lends authentic insight into human vulnerability, which shines in her diverse works spanning romance, historical fiction, contemporary drama, and short tales. Acclaimed titles include *Figs, Vines and Roses*, *The Liberty Bodice*, *Time's Pendulum Swings Again*, *Strawberry Moon*, *Joyful Pussy Cat Tales*, and *Bleeds When Broken*. Reviewers commend her graceful handling of sensitive topics, with one praising her ability to weave “compelling narratives around complex issues.” Inspired by family, faith, and her peaceful surroundings, Lilley connects warmly with readers through her blog and social media, offering stories that inspire reflection and hope.  Spy and Villain is a touching inspirational short story, its themes of vigilance, friendship, and faith-driven compassion affirming its appeal for readers seeking quick, meaningful reads with heart. Joy M. Lilley’s skillful blend of suspense and tenderness makes this compact gem a shining example of her compassionate storytelling legacy.
